fix: set _current_streaming_track_id eagerly to prevent duplicate detection

Track change was detected twice because _current_streaming_track_id
was only set inside get_audio_stream loop (async, delayed). The echo
from update_playing_status arrived before the stream loop picked up
the track, causing duplicate prebuffer start and wasted API call.

Fix: set _current_streaming_track_id immediately in _activate_playback
when track change is detected. The stream loop re-confirmation at
line 227 is kept as a safety guard.

commit.message:
feat: add switch/case/default statement (#41)

* feat: add switch/case/default statement

- Add COLON, CASE, DEFAULT, SWITCH token types
- Scan ':' as COLON; extend keyword trie for case/default/switch
- Store switch subject as hidden local '(switch)' to avoid DUP opcode
- Each case arm: GET_LOCAL + EQUAL + JUMP_IF_FALSE chain, no fall-through
- Multi-value arms (case 1, 2:) compile to an equality-chain
- Reuse LoopContext with start=-1 as switch sentinel for break/continue
- continue skips switch contexts to find nearest enclosing real loop
- emitLoopCleanup(int targetLocalCount) parameterized for correct cleanup
- Add SwitchTest suite (12 tests) covering all invariants

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add switch/case/default to language specification

- spec/README.md: add switch to the "Relationship to Book Lox" table
- spec/01-lexical.md: add COLON token; add case, default, switch keywords;
  update break note to mention switch
- spec/04-semantics.md: add switch statement section (no fall-through,
  multi-value arms, break/continue interaction); update break and continue
  descriptions to reflect switch context

* docs: add switch/case/default grammar rule to 02-syntax.md

Add switchStmt, switchArm, caseArm, and defaultArm productions to the
EBNF grammar, wire switchStmt into the statement rule, and add a Notes
section explaining the at-most-one-default constraint.

* fix: enforce default-last and document short-circuit in switch

Spec (02-syntax.md):
- Restructure switchStmt grammar to encode default-last directly:
  caseArm* defaultArm? (drop the intermediate switchArm non-terminal)
- Update Notes: default must be final arm; case-after-default is a
  static error

Spec (04-semantics.md):
- Clarify that case-value evaluation short-circuits on first match;
  remaining values in the arm and all subsequent arms are not evaluated
- Add "Side effects" callout warning against relying on unmatched
  case expressions
- Add "default placement" rule: case after default is a static error

Compiler (compiler.cpp):
- Emit a compile error if a case arm follows default

Test (test_control_flow.cpp):
- Add CaseAfterDefaultIsError to SwitchTest suite

commit.message:
Ⓜ feat: add configurable soft-delete mode for message deletion (#41)

Add a new deleteMode parameter to the delete-message action that supports
three modes:

- hard (default): permanently delete the message (existing behavior)
- soft: replace message content with a tombstone marker via edit
- block: refuse deletion entirely with an error

The tombstone text defaults to "~~This message was removed by an agent~~"
and can be customized via the deleteTombstone parameter or
TEAMS_DELETE_TOMBSTONE env var.

Closes #27

feat: implement automated monthly releases with YY-MM-patchlevel versioning (#7468)

* feat: implement automated monthly releases with YYYY-MM-patchlevel versioning

- Add release-monthly.yml workflow with scheduled (1st @ 08:00 UTC) and manual triggers
- Version scheme: YYYY-MM-patchlevel (e.g., 2026-04-0, 2026-04-1)
- Patchlevel auto-increments within same month, resets on month change
- Automatic git operations: commit, tag, push
- Automatic GitHub Release creation with auto-generated notes
- Automatic npm publication
- Update osrm-backend.yml to trigger on new tag pattern v[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]+
- Update CMakeLists.txt to support both new and legacy semver formats
- Update package.json to version 2026-04-0
- Update docs/releasing.md with new release process documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: unify versioning to single semver format

Instead of dual-format versioning (YYYY-MM-patchlevel for git tags,
(YYYY-2000).MM.patchlevel for npm), use a single unified format:

- Git tags: v26.4.0 (matching npm package version exactly)
- npm packages: 26.4.0
- No format conversion needed in release workflow

Changes:
- release-monthly.yml: Calculate version directly in semver format
- CMakeLists.txt: Remove YYYY-MM-patchlevel format handling
- version.hpp.in: Simplify to single semver format
- docs/releasing.md: Document unified versioning approach

Benefits:
- Eliminate format mismatch between git tags and npm
- Simpler release workflow (no conversion step needed)
- Single source of truth for version format
- Clearer documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add version history section explaining transition from v6.x to monthly scheme

Add 'Version History' section documenting:
- Previous scheme: Traditional semver (v6.0, v6.0.1, etc.)
  - Ended in December 2025 with v6.0.0 release
  - Manual release process

- New scheme: Monthly date-based versioning (starting 2026)
  - First release: v26.1.0 (January 2026)
  - Automated monthly releases on 1st of month at 08:00 UTC
  - Year offset format: YYYY-2000 (e.g., 2026 → 26)

Provides clear context for users transitioning between schemes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct CMake semver regex pattern for version matching

CMake's MATCHES operator does not support {1,2} quantifier syntax.
Changed regex from '[0-9]{1,2}' to '[0-9]+' to properly match version
numbers in both zero-padded (26.04.0) and non-padded (26.4.0) formats.

This fixes the build error:
  "Version from package.json cannot be parsed... But found: 26.4.0"

The regex now correctly matches:
- 26.4.0 (non-padded month)
- 26.04.0 (zero-padded month)
- 6.0.0 (legacy semver)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review feedback on automated release workflow

Critical fixes:
- Fix OSRM_VERSION CMake assignment to use variable value not literal string
- Restore legacy semver format support (with prerelease/build metadata)
- Enforce month 1-12 in CMake regex (no invalid months like 13)
- Enforce month 1-12 and no leading zeros in version_override validation
- Remove zero-padding from month calculation (use %-m not %m)
- Update package-lock.json in addition to package.json during releases

Improvements:
- Clearer CMake comments explaining both version formats
- Documentation clarifies git tag prefix (v) vs npm unprefixed format
- Documentation clarifies month is 1-12 without leading zeros (not MM)
- Ensure version_override rejects invalid formats like 26.00.0 or 26.13.0

The workflow now correctly:
- Calculates versions like 26.4.0 for April 2026 (not 26.04.0)
- Validates month to be in range 1-12
- Updates both package.json and package-lock.json consistently
- Generates correct OSRM_VERSION in runtime and pkg-config

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: implement robust CI polling before npm publish

Prevent npm publish from running before prebuilt binaries are available
by implementing proper CI workflow monitoring.

The osrm-backend.yml workflow (triggered by tag push) builds and uploads
prebuilt binaries to GitHub Release assets. If npm is published before
these binaries are available, subsequent installs via node-pre-gyp will fail.

Changes:
- Authenticate gh CLI with GH_TOKEN (github.token)
- Query osrm-backend.yml workflow runs with commit SHA filter
- Find the run matching the tag's commit SHA specifically
- Monitor status and conclusion fields
- Wait up to 5 minutes for completion
- Only proceed to npm publish if conclusion is 'success'
- Fail fast if CI workflow fails or times out

This ensures:
- Binaries are available before npm knows about the version
- npm installs will find prebuilt binaries in release assets
- Failed CI builds prevent broken npm releases
- Manual/scheduled releases don't race and fail mysteriously

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Ship libbearssl.a in Linux release bundles for TLS support

Release CI now builds BearSSL from the submodule during Linux
packaging (293 .c files → libbearssl.a ~2-3MB) and injects it into
the tarball at toolchain/lib/libbearssl.a.

find_bearssl_lib() now searches:
1. deps/laststanding/bearssl/build/ (dev mode)
2. <exe-dir>/toolchain/lib/ (release bundle)
3. <exe-dir>/lib/ (minimal install)

This makes TLS work out-of-the-box on Linux after installing from
the release tarball. Windows uses SChannel (no BearSSL needed).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
